name: JumpCloud
description: Cloud directory platform combining identity, SSO, MFA and device management. Yields user and group directory reports, MFA and conditional access policy evidence, device policy status and Directory Insights activity events.

steps.action: Sign in to the JumpCloud Admin Portal (console.jumpcloud.com) and navigate to Insights > Reports
steps.security_note: Use a least-privilege Read Only administrator for evidence collection, not an Administrator with Billing account

steps.action: Run the Users to Devices report and export it as CSV to evidence the user directory, attributes and device associations
steps.audit_note: The export reflects live directory state at generation time — save with a date-stamped filename

steps.action: Navigate to SECURITY MANAGEMENT > Conditional Policies and capture dated screenshots of each MFA and conditional access policy, showing status, conditions and assigned targets
steps.audit_note: Include the policy enabled/disabled status and the capture date in each screenshot so reviewers can tie evidence to the period

steps.action: Navigate to DEVICE MANAGEMENT > Policy Management and record the device policies in force (names, OS targets, applied groups) as a screenshot or export
Open the Directory Insights activity log, filter events to the collection period (e.g. admin console logins, SSO authentications) and export the results
steps.permissions_required: Read Only admin role (Directory Insights requires an eligible plan)
steps.audit_note: JumpCloud retains Directory Insights events for 90 days — collect well within that window to avoid unrecoverable gaps

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Directory exports contain personal data — handle in line with your data protection obligations and restrict repository access

steps.action: In Insights > Reports, run each evidence report (e.g. Users to Devices), apply the filters and columns you need, and save it as a Saved Report
From the Saved Reports tab, use Actions > Schedule Report to set the sending frequency, time, timezone and output format to match your evidence cadence
steps.audit_note: Scheduled generation removes reliance on individuals remembering to run reports

steps.action: Add scheduled OS Patch Management and Browser Patch Management Policy reports to evidence device policy compliance status across the fleet
steps.audit_note: Patch policy reports include per-device policy status and last-contact timestamps, evidencing both policy deployment and effectiveness

steps.action: Limit report notification recipients to authorised compliance personnel
steps.security_note: Directory and device reports contain personal and configuration data — do not deliver them to shared or external mailboxes

steps.action: Each cycle, download the generated reports from the Report History tab and file them in the evidence repository — download links are only valid for 7 days
steps.audit_note: Collect within the 7-day link validity window and verify a report exists for every scheduled period; a missing period is a collection gap

steps.action: Create a dedicated service account administrator with the Read Only role and API access enabled, then generate its API key via the account initials menu > My API Key, setting an expiration (e.g. 90 days)
steps.permissions_required: Administrator with Billing role to enable API access for the service account
steps.security_note: The API key inherits the admin's role permissions and grants broad access to your directory — use a Read Only service account, store the key in a secrets manager and rotate it at expiry

steps.action: Collect activity events by calling POST https://api.jumpcloud.com/insights/directory/v1/events with the x-api-key header and a body such as {"service": ["all"], "start_time": "<last-run-timestamp>"}
steps.permissions_required: API key from an admin with Directory Insights access
steps.audit_note: Directory Insights retains 90 days of events — daily pulls with overlapping time windows guarantee no events are lost

steps.action: Export directory state by calling GET https://console.jumpcloud.com/api/systemusers (API v1) for users, paging through all results
steps.permissions_required: API key with Read Only role

steps.action: Export groups and device policies via API v2: GET https://console.jumpcloud.com/api/v2/usergroups and GET https://console.jumpcloud.com/api/v2/policies
steps.permissions_required: API key with Read Only role
steps.audit_note: Point-in-time policy exports evidence configuration state; pair them with Directory Insights events to evidence change history

steps.action: Schedule the collection script daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, writing dated JSON/CSV outputs to the evidence repository, with error handling that alerts the compliance team on failed runs
steps.security_note: Never embed the x-api-key in source code or CI logs — inject it at runtime from the secrets manager

steps.action: Validate each run: compare user counts and daily event volume against the previous run's baseline and flag unexplained drops
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: JumpCloud Continuous Evidence Pipeline via SIEM Integration

steps.action: JumpCloud does not offer native webhooks or push streaming for Directory Insights — the closest managed option is a SIEM connector or JumpCloud's serverless collection app polling the Directory Insights API on a near-real-time interval
steps.audit_note: Document the polling interval in the evidence collection procedure; it defines your maximum detection and collection latency

steps.action: Configure your SIEM's JumpCloud integration (e.g. Datadog, Elastic, Sumo Logic connectors) with a dedicated Read Only service account API key to continuously ingest Directory Insights events
steps.permissions_required: Administrator with Billing role to enable API access; SIEM admin to configure the connector
steps.security_note: Grant the SIEM connector its own service account key so it can be rotated or revoked independently of other automation

steps.action: Track collection completeness metrics in the SIEM: events per day per service (SSO, LDAP, RADIUS, systems, directory), ingestion latency and poll success rate
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are the key evidence that the collection process itself is managed and monitored

steps.action: Reconcile streamed event counts periodically against direct queries to POST https://api.jumpcloud.com/insights/directory/v1/events for the same window, to detect silent connector gaps before the 90-day retention window closes
steps.audit_note: Reconciliation must happen well inside the 90-day retention period — after that, missed events are unrecoverable

steps.action: Build a collection health dashboard (events/day by service, latency, failed polls, coverage) and alert the compliance team when volume drops below baseline or the connector stalls
Run an automated quarterly review that produces a collection health report, flags SLA breaches or gaps, and files the report in the evidence repository
